skill.name: blockchain-developer
skill.description: Build production-ready Web3 applications, smart contracts, and decentralized systems. Implements DeFi protocols, NFT platforms, DAOs, and enterprise blockchain integrations.
skill.summary: Expert blockchain development guidance for smart contracts, DeFi protocols, and Web3 applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides guidance for blockchain development without any code execution capabilities, network access, filesystem operations, or external command execution. No security risks identified.

content.value_statement: Accelerate Web3 development with expert guidance for smart contracts, DeFi protocols, and decentralized applications. Get security-first code patterns and best practices across multiple blockchain ecosystems.
actual_capabilities: Design and implement smart contracts in Solidity, Rust, and Vyper
Build DeFi protocols including AMMs, lending platforms, and yield farming systems
Develop NFT marketplaces with ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token standards
Create DAO governance systems with token-weighted voting and proposal mechanisms
Integrate blockchain networks across Ethereum, Solana, Cosmos, and Layer 2 solutions
Implement security best practices including formal verification and vulnerability assessment
limitations: Cannot execute or deploy smart contracts directly
Does not interact with live blockchain networks or mainnet
Cannot perform real-time transaction monitoring or security audits
Limited to providing code patterns and architectural guidance

use_cases.title: DeFi Protocol Development
use_cases.description: Design and implement lending protocols, automated market makers, and yield farming platforms with security-first architecture
use_cases.target_user: DeFi developers building decentralized financial applications

use_cases.title: NFT Platform Creation
use_cases.description: Build NFT marketplaces with royalty distribution, cross-chain bridges, and on-chain metadata storage
use_cases.target_user: Web3 developers creating digital asset platforms

use_cases.title: Enterprise Blockchain Solutions
use_cases.description: Implement supply chain tracking, digital identity systems, and regulatory compliance frameworks for enterprise use
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise developers integrating blockchain into business operations

prompt_templates.title: Create Smart Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Solidity smart contract for [TOKEN_TYPE] with [FEATURES]. Include security best practices and gas optimization
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner developers building their first smart contract

prompt_templates.title: Build DeFi Protocol
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a DeFi [PROTOCOL_TYPE] protocol with collateralization, liquidation mechanisms, and oracle integration. Include security considerations
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate developers building lending or AMM protocols

prompt_templates.title: Implement Cross-Chain Solution
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a cross-chain bridge between [CHAIN_1] and [CHAIN_2] with security audits and relay mechanisms
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced developers building interoperable blockchain solutions

prompt_templates.title: Audit Smart Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a security audit of this smart contract: [CONTRACT_CODE]. Identify reentrancy, overflow, and access control vulnerabilities
prompt_templates.scenario: Security reviewers and auditors analyzing smart contracts

output_examples.input: Create an ERC-20 token with minting and burning capabilities
output_examples.output: Complete Solidity contract with OpenZeppelin imports, mint/burn functions, access control, and gas optimization patterns
Architecture proposal including voting contract, proposal structure, quorum requirements, and execution mechanisms

best_practices: Always implement comprehensive testing including unit tests, integration tests, and fuzzing before mainnet deployment
Use battle-tested libraries like OpenZeppelin instead of implementing security-critical code from scratch
Conduct formal verification and professional security audits for contracts handling significant value
anti_patterns: Deploying未经测试的 contracts to mainnet without thorough testnet validation
Implementing custom cryptographic primitives instead of using established libraries
Hardcoding admin keys or critical parameters without multi-signature protection

faq.question: Which blockchain platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports Ethereum and EVM-compatible chains, Solana, Cosmos, Polkadot, NEAR, Cardano, Algorand, Hyperledger Fabric, and Bitcoin Lightning Network.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy smart contracts to mainnet?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides code patterns and architectural guidance only. You must use development tools like Hardhat, Foundry, or Anchor to deploy contracts yourself.

faq.question: Does this skill perform security audits?
faq.answer: This skill provides security guidance and vulnerability assessment patterns but does not replace professional security audits. Always conduct formal audits for production contracts.

faq.question: Can I use this for enterprise blockchain solutions?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers enterprise blockchain patterns including Hyperledger Fabric, private networks, supply chain tracking, and regulatory compliance frameworks.

faq.question: What programming languages are covered?
faq.answer: Solidity for Ethereum, Rust for Solana/Cosmos/NEAR, Vyper for secure contracts, Haskell for Cardano, and PyTeal for Algorand.

faq.question: Does this help with Layer 2 solutions?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, zkSync, and cross-chain bridge development with security considerations.
